{"CommandLine":"python -c "
import openpyxl
import os

file_path = 'g:/My Drive/UCL/BSMA/me file/Archive/BSMA_Actual Coding Sheet_v2.xlsx'
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(file_path)
ws = wb.active

# Find the real last row by checking column B (Article ID)
real_last_row = 1
for r in range(4, ws.max_row + 1):
    if not ws.cell(row=r, column=2).value:
        real_last_row = r
        break

print(f'Real last row is {real_last_row}')

# Move the data from the bottom (max_row) to the real last row
# Wait, actually let's just delete everything after real_last_row and re-insert the 4 rows

ws.delete_rows(real_last_row, ws.max_row - real_last_row + 1)

new_rows = [
    # Row 1: Collaboration
    ['ALL', 'BSMA0002', 'BSMA0002.1', 'BSMA0002.1.1', '1 = Include', None, 
     'The public sector is increasingly collaborating with the private sector...', 
     'The Impact of Boundary Spanning by Public Managers on Collaboration and Infrastructure Project Performance', 
     'Public Performance & Management Review', 
     'Satheesh, S. A., Verweij, S., van Meerkerk, I., Busscher, T., & Arts, J.', 
     2023, '1 = Journal article', None, None, None, None, 
     '1 = Cross-sectional', None, 'Netherlands', None, '1 = No (domestic only)', 158, 999, 15.82, 999, 'Lead public managers', 
     13, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'New scale based on literature', None, 
     6, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'Koppenjan et al., 2020, 2022; Warsen, 2021', None, 
     'Boundary Spanning', 4.97, 1.48, 0.97, 
     'Collaboration', 5.15, 1.20, 0.91, 0.310, None],
    # Row 2: Performance
    ['ALL', 'BSMA0002', 'BSMA0002.1', 'BSMA0002.1.2', '1 = Include', None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, 
     13, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'New scale based on literature', None, 
     4, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'Koppenjan et al. (2020)', None, 
     'Boundary Spanning', 4.97, 1.48, 0.97, 
     'Performance', 5.69, 1.30, 0.86, 0.138, None],
    # Row 3: Years of Experience
    ['ALL', 'BSMA0002', 'BSMA0002.1', 'BSMA0002.1.3', '1 = Include', None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, 
     13, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'New scale based on literature', None, 
     999, 999, 999, '1 = Self', None, 999, None, 
     'Boundary Spanning', 4.97, 1.48, 0.97, 
     'Years of experience', 999, 999, 999, 0.271, None],
    # Row 4: Scope
    ['ALL', 'BSMA0002', 'BSMA0002.1', 'BSMA0002.1.4', '1 = Include', None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, None, 
     13, 1, 7, '1 = Self', None, 'New scale based on literature', None, 
     999, 999, 999, '1 = Self', None, 999, None, 
     'Boundary Spanning', 4.97, 1.48, 0.97, 
     'Scope', 999, 999, 999, 0.210, None]
]

for i, r in enumerate(new_rows):
    for col_idx, val in enumerate(r):
        ws.cell(row=real_last_row + i, column=col_idx + 1).value = val

wb.save(file_path)
print('Fixed successfully')
"","Cwd":"C:\temp","WaitMsBeforeAsync":500,"toolAction":"Fixing Excel blank rows","toolSummary":"Fix Excel"}
